summary: Self-reported health-related quality of life reflects meaningful patient-experienced outcomes linked to symptom burden and disease severity, which have been associated with mortality risks across studies. Given its incorporation within broader assessment models including psychological well-being and risk evaluation, it serves as an indirectly supportive prognostic indicator—despite lacking direct empirical statements—in non-small cell lung cancer.

Answer choice: A
